title: nephrodium filix-mas subsp. borreri
intro: Nephrodium filix-mas subsp. borreri, commonly known as the Borrer's fern or Borrer's lady fern, is a subspecies of the common lady fern (Nephrodium filix-mas) native to the British Isles. The Borrer's fern has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and soothing respiratory tract irritations. In herbal preparations, the Borrer's fern is often dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
